cwe: CWE-611 - Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference
CWE-827 - Improper Control of Document Type Definition

threat.title: XML External Entity (XXE) Injection in Spring Boot and ASP.NET Core APIs
threat.description: XXE attacks exploit XML parsers configured to process external entities, allowing attackers to read arbitrary files from the server, perform Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF), execute denial of service attacks, or exfiltrate sensitive data. This vulnerability commonly affects REST APIs that accept XML input without proper parser hardening.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies API endpoint accepting XML input (e.g., /api/v1/data with Content-Type: application/xml)
2. Sends malicious XML payload with external entity declaration pointing to /etc/passwd
3. Parser resolves external entity and includes file contents in response
4. Attacker escalates attack using SSRF to access internal services (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/)
5. Retrieves cloud credentials or internal API keys from metadata service
6. Uses stolen credentials to compromise cloud infrastructure or exfiltrate sensitive databases
prerequisites: API endpoint accepts XML input (REST, SOAP, or file upload)
XML parser configured with external entity processing enabled (DTD processing)
No input validation or Content-Type restrictions
Server has network access to internal services or file system read permissions

cve_references.description: Spring Data Commons XXE vulnerability when unmarshalling XML to Java objects using XMLBeam
Spring Framework OXM XXE vulnerability in Spring's XML-to-Object mapping with external entities enabled by default
Microsoft SharePoint XXE vulnerability allowing remote code execution via crafted XML

real_world_breaches.impact: XXE vulnerability in Facebook's careers portal allowed reading of internal files and SSRF attacks. Researcher awarded $33,500 bug bounty.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: XML parser processing external entities in document upload feature without validation

real_world_breaches.impact: XXE vulnerability in Google Toolbar button editor allowed local file disclosure
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Unconfigured XML parser processing user-supplied XML configurations

bug_bounty_reports.summary: XXE vulnerability in API endpoint allowing /etc/passwd file read and SSRF to AWS metadata

security_research.title: OWASP XXE Prevention Cheat Sheet
security_research.key_finding: XXE is still prevalent in 2024, affecting 13% of tested applications. Parser defaults remain insecure in many frameworks.

security_research.title: A Deep Dive into XXE Injection
security_research.key_finding: XXE can be used for blind SSRF attacks even when direct output is not reflected in responses

mitigations.title: Disable External Entity Processing in Spring Boot XML Parsers
mitigations.description: Configure all XML parsers (DocumentBuilderFactory, SAXParserFactory, XMLInputFactory) to disable DTD processing and external entity resolution. This is the most effective defense against XXE attacks.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
validation_steps: Test with malicious XXE payload - should reject or sanitize
Run static analysis with FindSecBugs to detect insecure parsers
Check application.properties for spring.xml.* security settings

mitigations.title: Disable External Entity Processing in ASP.NET Core XML Parsers
mitigations.description: Configure XmlReader, XmlDocument, and XmlSerializer with secure settings to prevent XXE attacks. Use XmlReaderSettings with DtdProcessing.Prohibit and XmlResolver set to null.
nist_csf: PR.IP-2 - A System Development Life Cycle is implemented
validation_steps: Verify XmlReaderSettings.DtdProcessing = DtdProcessing.Prohibit in all code
Check that XmlResolver is set to null
Test with XXE payload containing <!DOCTYPE> - should be rejected
Run Security Code Scan in CI/CD pipeline
Review all usages of XmlDocument, XmlReader, and XmlSerializer

mitigations.title: Implement Input Validation and Content-Type Restrictions
mitigations.description: Apply defense-in-depth by validating XML structure against strict schemas, restricting Content-Type headers, and implementing request size limits. Use allowlists for acceptable XML elements and reject suspicious patterns.
validation_steps: Test with malformed XML - should reject
Test with DOCTYPE declaration - should block
Test with oversized XML (>1MB) - should reject
Verify Content-Type restrictions in API gateway
Test schema validation with invalid structure

indicators: Unexpected file access to /etc/passwd, /etc/hosts, or sensitive configuration files
XML requests containing <!DOCTYPE>, <!ENTITY>, SYSTEM, or PUBLIC keywords
Large XML payloads (>100KB) indicating entity expansion attacks
Error messages revealing file system paths or internal service URLs
Slow response times during XML processing (billion laughs DoS)

log_queries.query:
index=app_logs sourcetype=spring_boot
| search ("<!DOCTYPE" OR "<!ENTITY" OR "SYSTEM" OR "file://" OR "/etc/passwd")
| rex field=request_body "(?<xxe_pattern><!DOCTYPE[^>]*>|<!ENTITY[^>]*>)"
| stats count by src_ip, request_path, xxe_pattern, _time
| where count > 1
| table _time, src_ip, request_path, xxe_pattern, count

http.request.body.content: (*DOCTYPE* OR *ENTITY* OR *SYSTEM* OR *file://*) 
AND http.request.mime_type: "application/xml"
| stats count by source.ip, url.path, http.response.status_code

// Detect XXE attack patterns in application logs
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where CsHost contains "api" and CsMethod == "POST"
| where ScStatus in (200, 400, 500)
| extend RequestBody = parse_json(tostring(CsBytes))
| where RequestBody contains "DOCTYPE" or RequestBody contains "ENTITY" or RequestBody contains "SYSTEM"
| summarize AttackCount = count() by CIp, CsUriStem, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where AttackCount > 3
| order by AttackCount desc

fields @timestamp, @message, sourceIPAddress, userAgent
| filter @message like /(?i)(<!DOCTYPE|<!ENTITY|SYSTEM|file:\/\/|etc\/passwd)/
| filter requestContext.httpMethod = "POST" and requestContext.path like /\/api\//
| stats count() as xxe_attempts by sourceIPAddress, requestContext.path
| filter xxe_attempts > 2
| sort xxe_attempts desc

tools.name: Datadog Application Security
tools.usage: Monitors XML parsing operations and blocks XXE attempts in real-time

tools.name: Falco (Kubernetes)
tools.usage: Detects file reads of /etc/passwd or cloud metadata access from application pods
